explanation.title: Arranging Elements in One Direction with Flexbox
explanation.content:
# CSS Flexbox Before Flexbox, centering a div vertically in CSS was famously difficult. Flexbox (Flexible Box Layout) changed everything. It lets you arrange items in a row or column, distribute space between them, and align them — all in a few lines of CSS. ## Activating Flexbox Add `display: flex` to a **container** element. All its direct children become **flex items**: ```html
Item 1
Item 2
Item 3
``` ```css .container { display: flex; } ``` ## The Two Axes Flexbox works along two axes: - **Main axis** — the direction items flow (default: horizontal, left to right) - **Cross axis** — perpendicular to the main axis (default: vertical) `flex-direction` sets the main axis: | Value | Main axis direction | |---|---| | `row` | Left to right (default) | | `row-reverse` | Right to left | | `column` | Top to bottom | | `column-reverse` | Bottom to top | ## Alignment Properties ```css .container { display: flex; flex-direction: row; /* Main axis alignment */ justify-content: flex-start; /* default */ /* flex-end | center | space-between | space-around | space-evenly */ /* Cross axis alignment */ align-items: stretch; /* default */ /* flex-start | flex-end | center | baseline */ /* Wrap when items overflow */ flex-wrap: nowrap; /* default */ /* wrap | wrap-reverse */ gap: 16px; /* space between flex items */ } ``` ## Flex Item Properties ```css .item { flex: 1; /* shorthand: grow=1, shrink=1, basis=0 */ flex-grow: 1; /* how much extra space to absorb */ flex-shrink: 0; /* do not shrink when space is tight */ align-self: center; /* override container's align-items for this item only */ } ``` `flex: 1` on all items distributes available space equally. `flex: 2` on one item gives it twice as much space as the others. In the code example below, you will see `justify-content` and `align-items` demonstrated with a navigation bar and a centred hero section.

codeExample.explanation:
- `justify-content: space-between` on `nav` — pushes the logo to the left edge and links to the right edge
- `align-items: center` on `nav` — vertically centres both the logo and the link group in the nav bar
- `flex-direction: column` on `.hero` — stacks children vertically instead of horizontally
- `justify-content: center; align-items: center` on `.hero` — centres children both vertically and horizontally
- `gap: 16px` — adds uniform spacing between flex items without needing margins
- `flex: 1` on `.card` — each card grows equally to fill the available width

exercise.title: Build a Flexbox Pricing Row
exercise.instructions:
Create a row of three pricing cards using Flexbox. The container div must have display flex, gap 24px, and padding 40px. Each card (class 'plan') must use flex: 1, have a border, border-radius 10px, padding 24px, and text-align center. The middle card should have background-color #22c55e and color white to highlight it. Each card needs an h3 plan name and a p description.

Expected: three equal-width cards side by side, middle one green.

hints: 💡 Add display: flex; gap: 24px; padding: 40px; to .plans to activate Flexbox layout.
💡 Add flex: 1; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 10px; padding: 24px; text-align: center; to .plan.
💡 The .plan.featured rule targets elements that have both classes — no extra HTML changes needed.

questions.question: Which property do you add to a container to make it a flex container?
questions.explanation: display: flex applied to a container element activates Flexbox. All of its direct children automatically become flex items. flex: 1 is a property for flex items, not the container. flex-direction: row is a container property but only controls the direction of an already-activated flex container.

questions.question: What does justify-content: space-between do in a flex container?
options: Adds equal space around all items
Places the first item at the start, last at the end, with equal gaps between
Makes all items the same width
questions.explanation: justify-content: space-between distributes items along the main axis so the first item is flush with the start, the last item is flush with the end, and remaining space is distributed equally between the items. Compare with space-around (equal space on both sides of each item) and space-evenly (equal space between all items and edges).

questions.question: What does flex: 1 do when applied to multiple flex items?
options: Sets each item's width to 1 pixel
Makes each item take up one row
Allows each item to grow equally and share available space
Fixes each item at the first position
questions.explanation: flex: 1 is shorthand for flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0. When multiple items all have flex: 1, they share the available space equally. If one item has flex: 2 and two others have flex: 1, the first item takes half the space and the others share the remaining half.
